mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or already near zero, equity discount rates stay suppressed and valuation support persists, so S&P 500 returns over the next month are positive unless inflation is accelerating faster than the policy rate decline.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward returns are negative in most periods when FEDFUNDS is flat-to-down and CPIAUCSL monthly inflation is not accelerating, or if Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL increase above the prior month that also leaves FEDFUNDS below CPI inflation implies a negative real policy rate surprise, which supports multiple expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains below trailing 12m CPI inflation
failure_condition: Reject if triggered months produce negative 20d S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time, or if the interaction term loses significance while either proxy alone has no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE over a short window signals weakening household income and softer risk appetite, so S&P 500 returns over the next month turn negative when unemployment is clearly trending higher.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 20d returns are not negative on average after the trigger, or if triggered episodes show positive returns in more than 60% of cases over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS keeps rising while UNRATE stays low, labor income growth supports consumption and reduces recession odds, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 60d returns do not follow the joint PAYEMS-up and UNRATE-low condition, or if the interaction adds no incremental predictive value relative to either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: A broad decline in RSAFS after an earlier rise indicates demand deceleration in nominal spending, which should pressure earnings expectations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive prints
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive RSAFS declines are not followed by below-zero 20d returns on average, or if the signal’s Rank IC falls below 0.03 in any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An upswing in HOUST reflects renewed residential demand and a lower recession probability, which can improve cyclical earnings expectations and support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 2 straight months after a prior trough
failure_condition: Reject if post-trough HOUST rises do not precede positive 60d returns, or if the effect disappears once GDP growth is controlled for.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO improves while CPIAUCSL inflation is not accelerating, the market reads the move as better real activity without an overheating shock, which should raise S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if joint INDPRO-up and non-accelerating inflation periods fail to deliver positive 20d returns, or if the interaction term is not stronger than the single-series effects.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a negative shock to INDPRO relative to lagged output growth signals supply constraint stress and margin compression, so S&P 500 returns over the next week should be negative.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises by at least 0.50 percentage points within 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if short-horizon returns are not negative during tightening episodes, or if the combined INDPRO-FEDFUNDS condition does not outperform a null benchmark in walk-forward tests.
